name: ActionBar
description: A flexible horizontal bar that provides a standardized layout for arranging navigation elements, actions, and informational content within an application. Serves as a container for organizing UI elements like breadcrumbs, buttons, and dropdown menus into defined sections and groups. Exposes nested sub-components: ActionBar.Section, ActionBar.Group, ActionBar.Item, and ActionBar.Separator.
when_to_use: When building a page-level toolbar with breadcrumbs and action buttons
When you need to organize navigation elements into primary and secondary sections
When actions on the right and navigation on the left need to be laid out in a single horizontal bar
avoid: Do not use for inline content toolbars unrelated to page-level navigation — use a standalone toolbar or ButtonGroup instead
Do not use as a main sidebar navigation — use AsideHeader for that

props.name: aria-label
props.description: HTML aria-label attribute for accessibility.

props.name: children
props.description: ActionBar.Section, ActionBar.Group, ActionBar.Item, or ActionBar.Separator children.

props.name: className
props.description: HTML class attribute.

props.name: style
props.description: HTML style attribute.

props.name: qa
props.description: data-qa HTML attribute used for testing.

examples:
<ActionBar aria-label="Actions bar">
  <ActionBar.Section type="secondary">
    <ActionBar.Group>
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <Select value={['main']} options={[{content: 'main', value: 'main'}]} />
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
  </ActionBar.Section>
  <ActionBar.Section type="primary">
    <ActionBar.Group pull="left">
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <Breadcrumbs items={[{text: 'Root', action() {}}]} />
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
    <ActionBar.Group pull="right">
      <ActionBar.Item>
        <Button>Action</Button>
      </ActionBar.Item>
    </ActionBar.Group>
  </ActionBar.Section>
</ActionBar>

name: AsideHeader
description: A flexible and customizable collapsible sidebar navigation component. Provides a robust solution for creating intuitive navigation systems with a logo, top subheader items, a middle menu section, and a footer section. Supports pinned/unpinned states, custom backgrounds, header decoration, menu groups, drawer panels, and an optional top alert banner.
when_to_use: When building a desktop application layout that needs a persistent left sidebar navigation
When you need collapsible navigation with icon-only collapsed state and full expanded state
When the navigation requires custom branding, logos, and footer items
avoid: Do not use for mobile navigation — use MobileHeader instead
Do not use for tab-style horizontal navigation — use a Tabs component
If you need faster initial page content render, use PageLayout + PageLayoutAside instead of AsideHeader directly

props.name: pinned
props.description: Navigation visual state. When true, navigation is expanded (pinned open).

props.name: logo
props.description: Logo container including icon, title, and click handling.

props.name: menuItems
props.description: Items in the navigation middle section. Defaults to [].

props.name: subheaderItems
props.description: Items in the navigation top section under the Logo.

props.name: onChangePinned
props.description: Callback called when the navigation visual state changes.

props.name: renderContent
props.description: Function rendering the main content to the right of the AsideHeader.

props.name: renderFooter
props.type: (data: {size: number; isExpanded: boolean; isPinned: boolean; asideRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>}) => React.ReactNode | React.ReactNode[]
props.description: Function rendering the navigation bottom section.

props.name: renderFooterAfter
props.description: Renders additional content after footer items (e.g. user profile).

props.name: panelItems
props.description: Items for the Drawer component. Used to show additional information over main content.

props.name: topAlert
props.description: A container above the navigation based on the uikit Alert component.

props.name: headerDecoration
props.description: Color background of the top section with logo and subheader items. Defaults to false.

props.name: hideCollapseButton
props.description: Hides the CollapseButton. Defaults to false.

props.name: isCompactMode
props.description: When true, menu items use compact height. Defaults to false.

props.name: customBackground
props.description: Custom background for the AsideHeader (e.g. image or gradient).

props.name: customBackgroundClassName
props.description: Overrides default background container styles.

props.name: collapseTitle
props.description: CollapseButton title for collapsing navigation.

props.name: expandTitle
props.description: CollapseButton title for expanding navigation.

props.name: menuMoreTitle
props.description: Title for the additional element when menuItems don't fit.

props.name: menuGroups
props.description: Menu groups for collapsible sections.

props.name: defaultMenuGroups
props.description: Default menu groups state.

props.name: onMenuItemsChanged
props.description: Callback called when updating list of menuItems in AllPagesPanel.

props.name: onMenuGroupsChanged
props.description: Callback when menu groups collapsed state is updated.

props.name: onClosePanel
props.description: Callback called when closing a panel.

props.name: openModalSubscriber
props.description: Function that notifies AsideHeader about Modal visibility changes.

props.name: collapseButtonWrapper
props.type: (defaultButton: React.ReactNode, data: {isExpanded: boolean; onChangePinned?: (pinned: boolean) => void}) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Wrapper for CollapseButton allowing customization of the default button appearance.

props.name: className
props.description: HTML class attribute of the Logo.

props.name: qa
props.description: The value passed to data-qa attribute of the AsideHeader container.

examples:
<AsideHeader
  logo={{icon: LogoIcon, text: 'My App', href: '/'}}
  pinned={isPinned}
  onChangePinned={setIsPinned}
  menuItems={[
    {id: 'home', title: 'Home', icon: HomeIcon, current: true, onItemClick: handleClick},
    {id: 'settings', title: 'Settings', icon: SettingsIcon, onItemClick: handleClick},
  ]}
  renderContent={({size}) => <main style={{paddingLeft: size}}>Content</main>}
/>

name: AsideFallback
description: A placeholder component used as a Suspense fallback while the AsideHeader or PageLayoutAside is loading lazily. Renders a skeleton sidebar of the correct width to prevent layout shift during code splitting.
when_to_use: When using React.lazy to split the AsideHeader/PageLayoutAside into a separate chunk
When wrapping the aside navigation in a Suspense boundary
avoid: Do not use outside of a Suspense boundary
Do not use when AsideHeader is not lazy-loaded

name: Footer
description: The desktop page footer component that renders a logo, menu links, copyright, and an optional overflow button. Supports normal (white background) and clear (transparent, copyright-only) views.
when_to_use: When building desktop page layouts that need a bottom footer with logo, links, and copyright
When the footer needs to collapse overflow menu items behind a 'more' button
avoid: Do not use on mobile layouts — use MobileFooter instead
Do not use inside the AsideHeader sidebar — use renderFooter/FooterItem for that

props.name: copyright
props.description: Copyright text displayed in the footer.

props.name: className
props.description: Footer class name.

props.name: menuItems
props.description: List of footer menu items.

props.name: withDivider
props.description: Shows a top border on the footer.

props.name: moreButtonTitle
props.description: Title for the overflow 'more' button.

props.name: onMoreButtonClick
props.description: Click handler for the 'more' overflow button.

props.name: view
props.description: Footer appearance. 'normal' shows all elements; 'clear' is transparent with copyright only.

props.name: logo
props.description: Service logo properties.

props.name: logoWrapperClassName
props.description: The logo wrapper class name.

examples:
<Footer
  copyright={`© ${new Date().getFullYear()} My Service`}
  withDivider
  logo={{icon: LogoIcon, iconSize: 24, text: 'My Service'}}
  menuItems={[
    {text: 'About', href: '/about'},
    {text: 'Docs', href: '/docs'},
  ]}
/>

name: FooterItem
description: An individual item component used in the AsideHeader footer section. Provides consistent styling for footer action items including icons, tooltips, and bringForward support for highlighting over modals.
when_to_use: When adding items to the AsideHeader renderFooter section
When you need consistent styling for footer actions like support links, user profile, or settings
avoid: Do not use in the main menuItems section of AsideHeader — configure those as AsideHeaderItem objects instead

name: HotkeysPanel
description: A navigation panel that displays a searchable, grouped list of keyboard shortcuts for an application. Renders inside a Drawer component and optionally shows a search input to filter hotkeys by name.
when_to_use: When building a keyboard shortcut reference panel accessible via a hotkey or button
When you need to present grouped, searchable keyboard shortcuts to users
avoid: Do not use to manage hotkey bindings — it is display-only
Do not use as a general help panel with non-hotkey content

props.name: hotkeys
props.description: List of hotkey groups to display.

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the drawer is open.

props.name: title
props.description: The panel title.

props.name: togglePanelHotkey
props.description: The hotkey combination that toggles the panel, displayed in the title.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Close drawer handler.

props.name: filterable
props.description: Whether to show the search input. Defaults to true.

props.name: filterPlaceholder
props.description: Search input placeholder.

props.name: emptyState
props.description: No search results placeholder.

props.name: leftOffset
props.description: Drawer left offset. Defaults to 0.

props.name: topOffset
props.description: Drawer top offset. Defaults to 0.

props.name: platform
props.description: Platform for hotkey display format. Defaults to auto-detect.

props.name: className
props.description: Drawer class name.

props.name: style
props.description: Custom styles for the drawer.

props.name: container
props.description: DOM element to render the drawer into.

props.name: hideVeil
props.description: Whether to hide the drawer overlay (veil).

examples:
<HotkeysPanel
  open={isPanelOpen}
  onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}
  title="Keyboard Shortcuts"
  togglePanelHotkey="?"
  hotkeys={[
    {
      title: 'Navigation',
      items: [
        {title: 'Go to home', value: 'g h'},
        {title: 'Go to settings', value: 'g s'},
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>

name: Logo
description: A logo component used inside the AsideHeader navigation. Renders a clickable icon (wrapped in a button or anchor) with an optional text label that transitions in/out when the sidebar expands or collapses.
when_to_use: When configuring the logo prop of AsideHeader or PageLayoutAside
When you need a brand logo with icon, text, and link in the sidebar
avoid: Do not use directly in page content — it is designed for AsideHeader sidebar context
Do not use for logos in the Footer — pass LogoProps to the Footer component directly

props.name: text
props.description: Logo text, displayed only when pinned/expanded. Can be a render function.

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon component data for the logo.

props.name: iconSrc
props.description: HTML src attribute for an img tag icon.

props.name: href
props.description: URL the logo links to.

props.name: target
props.description: HTML target attribute. Defaults to '_self'.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Callback when the user clicks the logo.

props.name: iconSize
props.description: Width and height of the icon. Defaults to 24.

props.name: textSize
props.description: Text font size. Defaults to 15.

props.name: wrapper
props.description: Wrapper function wrapping icon and text in two separated containers.

props.name: className
props.description: HTML class attribute.

name: MobileFooter
description: The mobile page footer component, equivalent to Footer but optimized for mobile viewports. Shares the same props interface as Footer.
when_to_use: When building mobile page layouts that need a bottom footer with logo, links, and copyright
avoid: Do not use on desktop layouts — use Footer instead

name: MobileLogo
description: A logo component designed for use inside the MobileHeader. Renders a clickable icon and text label as an anchor tag (or wraps them via a custom wrapper function). Unlike the desktop Logo, it does not animate based on sidebar expand state — it always shows both icon and text.
when_to_use: When building a mobile header and need the brand logo with icon and text as a link
Inside MobileHeader's logo slot for mobile layouts
avoid: Do not use in desktop AsideHeader — use Logo instead
Do not use for logos outside of navigation contexts

props.name: isExpanded
props.description: Whether the burger menu is expanded; passed to the wrapper function when used

props.name: text
props.description: Logo text label or a render function returning React content

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon component data for the logo icon

props.name: iconSrc
props.description: HTML src for an img tag logo icon

props.name: iconSize
props.description: Icon width and height in px (default 32)

props.name: textSize
props.description: Text font size in px (default 20)

props.name: href
props.description: URL the logo links to (default '/')

props.name: target
props.description: Anchor target attribute (default '_self')

props.name: wrapper
props.description: Custom wrapper replacing the anchor; receives icon+text node and isExpanded

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class

name: MobileHeader
description: A mobile navigation header component that renders a burger menu button, logo, and optional side items. Manages a burger menu panel (Drawer), additional custom panel items, and an optional overlap panel. Supports dispatching custom events via CustomEvent on the component's DOM node to open/close panels programmatically.
when_to_use: When building mobile or responsive application layouts that need a top navigation header
When you need a burger menu with customizable navigation links for mobile users
avoid: Do not use on desktop layouts — use AsideHeader for desktop sidebar navigation
Do not use when you only need a simple top bar without a burger panel

props.name: logo
props.description: Logo configuration for the mobile header.

props.name: burgerMenu
props.description: Configuration for the burger menu panel including items and footer renderer.

props.name: panelItems
props.description: Additional panel items rendered as Drawer panels.

props.name: topAlert
props.description: Alert banner above the mobile header.

props.name: renderContent
props.description: Function rendering the main content below the header.

props.name: sideItemRenderContent
props.description: Renders content in the right side slot of the header (e.g. notifications icon).

props.name: onEvent
props.description: Handles MobileHeader inner events (panel open/close).

props.name: onClosePanel
props.description: Callback called when a panel closes.

props.name: overlapPanel
props.description: Configuration for an overlap panel (full-screen overlay).

props.name: burgerCloseTitle
props.description: Accessibility title for the burger close button.

props.name: burgerOpenTitle
props.description: Accessibility title for the burger open button.

props.name: className
props.description: HTML class attribute.

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: HTML class attribute for the content area.

examples:
<MobileHeader
  logo={{icon: LogoIcon, text: 'My App', href: '/'}}
  burgerMenu={{
    items: [
      {id: 'home', title: 'Home', icon: HomeIcon, onItemClick: () => navigate('/')},
      {id: 'settings', title: 'Settings', icon: SettingsIcon, onItemClick: () => navigate('/settings')},
    ],
  }}
  renderContent={({size}) => <main style={{paddingTop: size}}>Page content</main>}
/>

name: PageLayout
description: A layout container that composes the AsideHeader navigation with page content. Manages the pinned state and provides PageLayout.Content as a sub-component. Used as the recommended pattern when content rendering performance matters, enabling lazy loading of the aside navigation separately from the main content.
when_to_use: When building application layouts where navigation and content should be rendered as independent React trees for performance
When using React.lazy to code-split the aside navigation
avoid: Do not use when you can use AsideHeader with renderContent — PageLayout is for advanced optimization scenarios

name: PageLayoutAside
description: The aside panel component used within PageLayout. Accepts the same props as AsideHeader and renders the sidebar navigation. Used together with PageLayout and PageLayout.Content to enable rendering optimization via code splitting.
when_to_use: As the aside navigation inside a PageLayout when using React.lazy for sidebar code splitting
When migrating from AsideHeader to the optimized PageLayout pattern
avoid: Do not use outside of a PageLayout context
Do not use when AsideHeader alone is sufficient

name: Settings
description: A settings panel layout component that provides single-level and two-level grouping of settings pages with built-in filtering by title. Exposes nested sub-components Settings.Group, Settings.Page, Settings.Section, and Settings.Item. Supports both normal (desktop) and mobile views.
when_to_use: When building an application settings page with multiple categories and items
When you need searchable, grouped settings with a left menu and right content layout
When settings need to work in both desktop and mobile views
avoid: Do not use for single-setting or single-option forms — use a simple form instead
Do not use when you need non-standard settings layout not based on title/content pairs

props.name: children
props.description: Settings.Group and/or Settings.Page children.

props.name: title
props.description: Settings panel title. Defaults to 'Settings'.

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows loading state.

props.name: renderLoading
props.description: Custom content for loading state.

props.name: renderNotFound
props.description: Empty panel content when no items match search.

props.name: initialPage
props.description: Initial page in '/groupId/pageId' format.

props.name: view
props.description: Changes view for mobile. Defaults to 'normal'.

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Page change handler.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Settings close handler.

props.name: filterPlaceholder
props.description: Filter placeholder text. Defaults to 'Search settings'.

props.name: emptyPlaceholder
props.description: Filter empty text. Defaults to 'No results found'.

examples:
<Settings onClose={handleClose}>
  <Settings.Page title="Appearance">
    <Settings.Section title="Theme">
      <Settings.Item title="Color scheme">
        <Select options={themeOptions} />
      </Settings.Item>
    </Settings.Section>
  </Settings.Page>
  <Settings.Page title="Notifications">
    <Settings.Section title="Email">
      <Settings.Item title="Send digests">
        <Switch checked={digestEnabled} onUpdate={setDigestEnabled} />
      </Settings.Item>
    </Settings.Section>
  </Settings.Page>
</Settings>

name: Title
description: A panel title component that renders a subheader-3 heading with an optional close button. Used to provide consistent heading styles for navigation panels and drawers.
when_to_use: When adding a title to a navigation panel or drawer that may need a close action
When you need a consistent heading style matching other navigation components
avoid: Do not use for general page headings — use uikit Text or heading tags instead
Do not use inside Settings — that component manages its own title

props.name: children
props.description: Title text content.

props.name: onClose
props.description: If provided, renders a close (X) button.

props.name: hasSeparator
props.description: Adds a horizontal separator below the title.

props.name: closeTitle
props.description: Aria label for the close button.

props.name: closeIconSize
props.description: Size of the close icon. Defaults to 23.

name: TopAlert
description: An alert banner displayed above the AsideHeader or MobileHeader navigation. Based on the uikit Alert component, it supports warning/info/danger themes, custom content via a render function, and optional close behavior. Reading the CSS variable `--gn-top-alert-height` allows layouts to account for the banner height.
when_to_use: When displaying site-wide announcements, warnings, or call-to-action banners above navigation
When users need persistent contextual warnings visible on all pages
avoid: Do not use for inline or contextual alerts within page content — use uikit Alert directly
Do not configure directly — pass a TopAlertProps object to the topAlert prop of AsideHeader or MobileHeader

props.name: alert
props.description: Alert configuration object or render function.

props.name: className
props.description: HTML class attribute.

props.name: mobileView
props.description: Adapts rendering for mobile layout. Defaults to false.

name: useForwardRef
description: A utility hook that synchronizes a forwarded ref (callback or object ref) with an internal ref created by useRef. Returns the internal ref object for use within the component.
when_to_use: When building components that accept a forwarded ref and also need to use the ref internally
avoid: Do not use when React.forwardRef + useRef is sufficient without needing both an internal and external ref simultaneously
signature: function useForwardRef<T>(ref: ((instance: T | null) => void) | React.MutableRefObject<T | null> | null, initialValue?: any): React.MutableRefObject<T>

parameters.name: initialValue
parameters.description: Initial value for the internal ref. Defaults to null.

name: useOverflowingHorizontalListItems
description: A hook that calculates which items in a horizontal list fit within a container and which overflow. Uses ResizeObserver to respond to container size changes and returns separate arrays for visible and hidden items.
when_to_use: When building a horizontal menu or toolbar where items that don't fit should be hidden behind a 'more' button
When the container can resize and the visible item count needs to update dynamically
avoid: Do not use for vertical lists — it measures horizontal widths only
Do not use when all items should always be visible regardless of space
signature: function useOverflowingHorizontalListItems<ItemType>({ containerRef, items, itemSelector, moreButtonWidth }: UseOverflowingContainerListItemsProps<ItemType>): { visibleItems: ItemType[]; hiddenItems: ItemType[]; measured: boolean }

parameters.name: containerRef
parameters.description: Ref to the list container element.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: Array of items to evaluate for overflow.

parameters.name: itemSelector
parameters.description: CSS selector for individual item elements within the container.

parameters.name: moreButtonWidth
parameters.description: Width reserved for the overflow 'more' button. Defaults to 0.

return_type: { visibleItems: ItemType[]; hiddenItems: ItemType[]; measured: boolean }
